response: convert the art collection tax by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in an effective rate of 19% on the $2 million value, which yields a tax of $380,000. convert the original real estate tax calculation by applying the progressive rates to the $5 million value, totaling $1.15 million in taxes for the first scenario. convert the securities portfolio tax by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value before applying the 15% rate, resulting in a tax of $435,000. convert the initial total estate tax liability by summing the individual asset taxes to reach a final figure of $1.965 million. convert the real estate valuation to reflect the 10% underestimation correction, raising the portfolio value from $5 million to $5.5 million. convert the new real estate tax calculation by applying the progressive brackets to the adjusted $5.5 million value, which results in a tax of $1.25 million. convert the updated total estate tax liability by combining the unchanged art and securities taxes with the new real estate tax to arrive at a final total of $2.065 million.
